region.name: State of California
region.description: California is the US's 3rd largest state by land mass and has its largest population, with over 39 million residents.

indicators.name: Connectivity (Internet Access)
indicators.description: Percent of households without any internet subscription or access through cellphone-only
indicators.source: U.S. Census Bureau. (n.d.). California households without internet access [Data table].

indicators.name: Transportation (Transit Report Card)
indicators.description: Report card scores on vehicle miles traveled and transit access only
indicators.source: Transportation for America. (n.d.). Transit report card.

indicators.name: Literacy Rate
indicators.description: Percent of people 16-74 with literacy proficiency at or below Level 1
indicators.source: Institute of Education Sciences, National Center for Education Statistics.

indicators.name: Student Loan Debt
indicators.description: Percent of college graduates with student loan debt
indicators.source: The Institute for College Access & Success. (2021).

indicators.name: Energy Burden
indicators.description: Percent of households whose energy bill was >=10% of household income
indicators.source: Fisher, Sheehan & Colton. (2023). Home Energy Affordability Gap.

indicators.name: Energy Reliability
indicators.description: Percent of population living in counties with average total outage time of 6 hours or more per year
indicators.source: Murphy, P. & Kwoka, B. (in preparation). Equity-adjusted value of lost load.

indicators.name: Gender Pay Gap
indicators.description: Percent of men's median wage earned by women for full-time year-round work
indicators.source: U.S. Census Bureau. (2023). What is the gender wage gap in your state?

indicators.name: Racial Equity Index
indicators.description: Inclusion score based on racial gaps in economic vitality, readiness, and connectedness
indicators.source: PolicyLink & USC Equity Research Institute. (2024). Racial Equity Index.

indicators.name: Food Insecurity
indicators.description: Percent of households who live with food insecurity
indicators.source: USDA Economic Research Service. Food security in the U.S.

indicators.name: Vegetables per Day
indicators.description: Percent of adults who eat >=1 serving(s) of vegetables per day
indicators.source: California Department of Public Health. California community obesity profiles.

indicators.name: Healthcare Affordability
indicators.description: Percent of adults who delayed, skipped, or reduced their utilization of health care due to costs
indicators.source: California Health Care Foundation. Understanding California's health care affordability crisis.

indicators.name: Life Expectancy
indicators.description: Percent of population living in counties with life expectancy below OECD average (80.9 years)
indicators.source: County Health Rankings and Roadmaps. Life Expectancy in California. Year: 2023.

indicators.name: Homelessness
indicators.description: Point-in-time count of unhoused people
indicators.source: HUD. (2024). Continuum of care homeless populations report: California 2024.

indicators.name: Housing Cost Burden
indicators.description: Percent of renters with housing costs that exceed 30% of household income
indicators.source: Public Policy Institute of California. Californians and the housing crisis.

indicators.name: Poverty
indicators.description: Percent of people experiencing poverty defined by supplemental poverty rate (3-year average)
indicators.source: U.S. Census Bureau. (2024). Supplemental poverty measure.

indicators.name: Unemployment (U-6)
indicators.description: Percent of unemployed people including workers employed part-time for economic reasons and those marginally attached to the labor force
indicators.source: Bureau of Labor Statistics. (2024). Alternative measures of labor underutilization in California.

indicators.name: Police Scorecard
indicators.description: Averaged score for police funding, accountability, violence, and approach to law enforcement
indicators.source: Police Scorecard. California.

indicators.name: Violent Crime
indicators.description: Percent of CA people living in counties with a violent crime rate above 200 incidents per 100000 residents
indicators.source: California Department of Justice. OpenJustice: Crime data and statistics.

indicators.name: Registered Voters
indicators.description: Percent of eligible voters who are not registered to vote
indicators.source: California Secretary of State. (2024). Historical voter registration statistics.

indicators.name: Voter Turnout
indicators.description: Percent of eligible voter turnout
indicators.source: California Secretary of State. Historical Voter Registration and Participation.

indicators.name: Income Inequality
indicators.description: Percent of people living in counties with a Gini Index below 0.30
indicators.source: U.S. Census Bureau. Gini Index of Income Inequality, ACS 5-Year Estimates.

indicators.name: Report Not Happy
indicators.description: Percent of people who report they are not too happy
indicators.source: Baldassare, M., et al. (2023). PPIC Statewide Survey.

indicators.name: Sanitation Access
indicators.description: Percent of people who lack basic access to sanitation
indicators.source: Capone, D., et al. (2020). Water and sanitation in urban America.

indicators.name: Water Quality
indicators.description: Percent of people who receive water from systems that do not meet drinking water quality standards
indicators.source: California State Water Resources Control Board. SAFER dashboard.

indicators.name: Ozone Pollution
indicators.description: Percent of air basins in violation of the national ozone standard of 0.070 ppm
indicators.source: California Air Resources Board. Air quality and emissions data.

indicators.name: Particulate Matter
indicators.description: Percent of air basins in violation of annual average national PM2.5 standard (9 micrograms/m3)
indicators.source: California Air Resources Board. Air quality and emissions data.

indicators.name: At-Risk Species
indicators.description: Percent of animal / plant species at risk of extinction
indicators.source: NatureServe. (2023). Biodiversity in focus: United States edition.

indicators.name: Protected Area
indicators.description: Percent protected land / sea area
indicators.source: 30x30 California. (2024). Pathways to 30x30.

indicators.name: Toxic Chemicals Released
indicators.description: Percent of toxic chemicals released out of total toxic chemicals produced per year
indicators.source: U.S. EPA. (2024). Toxics release inventory: California fact sheet.

indicators.name: Toxic Pesticides Applied
indicators.description: Percent of total pesticides applied per year which are categorized as high-risk toxins
indicators.source: California Department of Pesticide Regulation. (2024). Pesticide Use Annual Report.

indicators.name: Greenhouse Gas Footprint
indicators.description: Greenhouse gas footprint per capita
indicators.source: EPA. (2024). Consumption-based GHG emissions for CA 2012-2019.

indicators.name: Renewable Electricity
indicators.description: Percent of electricity from renewables (solar, wind, and geothermal)
indicators.source: U.S. Energy Information Administration. (2024). California state energy profile.

indicators.name: Blue Water Footprint
indicators.description: Blue water footprint per capita
indicators.source: Ingwersen, W., et al. (2024). USEEIO State Models v1.0.

indicators.name: Groundwater
indicators.description: Percent of groundwater pumped from basins currently designated as critically overdrafted
indicators.source: California Natural Resources Agency. (2021). California groundwater update 2020.

indicators.name: Ecological Footprint
indicators.description: Ecological footprint per capita
indicators.source: Global Footprint Network. (2015). State of the States Report.

indicators.name: Forest Cover
indicators.description: Percent of land with forest cover
indicators.source: California Natural Resources Agency. (2024). California's Nature-Based Solutions Climate Targets.

indicators.name: Eutrophication Potential
indicators.description: Eutrophication potential footprint per capita
indicators.source: Ingwersen, W., et al. (2024). USEEIO State Models v1.0.

indicators.name: Nitrogen Leached
indicators.description: Nitrogen leached to groundwater per year
indicators.source: Tomich, T., et al. (2016). The California nitrogen assessment.

indicators.name: Ocean pH
indicators.description: Change in pH compared to preindustrial levels
indicators.source: Osborne, E., et al. (2020). Decadal variability in ocean acidification in the California Current.

indicators.name: SO2-eq Footprint
indicators.description: Acidification potential footprint per capita
indicators.source: Ingwersen, W., et al. (2024). USEEIO State Models v1.0.

indicators.name: Ozone Layer
indicators.description: Global average thickness of ozone layer
indicators.source: World Meteorological Organization. (2022). Scientific Assessment of Ozone Depletion: 2022.

indicators.name: Ozone-Depleting Substances
indicators.description: Compliance with Montreal Protocol on Ozone-Depleting Substances
indicators.source: California Air Resources Board. (2014). Ozone-depleting substances projects.
